    span { loc = "location", message = "Invalid shape ['~rowCount', '~colCount'] for cooperative matrix '~matrixUse'." }
)

fatal(
    "cooperative-matrix-unsupported-capture",
    51701,
    "'CoopMat.MapElement' per-element function cannot capture buffers, resources or any opaque type values",
    span { loc = "location", message = "'CoopMat.MapElement' per-element function cannot capture buffers, resources or any opaque type values. Consider pre-loading the content of any referenced buffers into a local variable before calling 'CoopMat.MapElement', or moving any referenced resources to global scope." }
)


-- Load semantic checking diagnostics (part 17) - Standalone notes for cross-referencing
-- (inlined from slang-diagnostics-semantic-checking-17.lua)

--
-- -1 - Notes that decorate another diagnostic.
--
-- These are standalone notes used after various error diagnostics to point
-- to related locations (definitions, declarations, etc.)
--

-- Note: These notes use Decl* which provides both the name (via getName()) and
-- location (via loc). The diagnostic system will extract the name for display.

standalone_note(
    "see-definition-of",
    -1,
    "see definition of '~decl:Decl'",
    span { loc = "decl:Decl" }
)

standalone_note(
    "see-definition-of-conversion-function",
    -1,
    "see definition of the conversion function '~decl:Decl'",
    span { loc = "decl:Decl" }
)

standalone_note(
    "see-definition-of-constraint",
    -1,
    "see definition of the unsatisfied constraint '~decl:Decl'",
    span { loc = "decl:Decl" }
)

standalone_note(
    "see-definition-of-struct",
    -1,
    "see definition of struct '~name'",
    span { loc = "location" }
)

standalone_note(
    "see-constant-buffer-definition",
    -1,
    "see constant buffer definition.",
    span { loc = "location" }
)

-- Note: seeUsingOf takes both a decl (for name) and a location (for where it's used)
-- This allows pointing to the call site while showing the name of what's being used
standalone_note(
    "see-using-of",
    -1,
    "see using of '~decl:Decl'",
    span { loc = "location" }
)

standalone_note(
    "see-call-of-func",
    -1,
    "see call to '~name'",
    span { loc = "location" }
)

standalone_note(
    "see-previous-definition",
    -1,
    "see previous definition",
    span { loc = "location" }
)

standalone_note(
    "see-previous-definition-of",
    -1,
    "see previous definition of '~decl:Decl'",
    span { loc = "decl:Decl" }
)

standalone_note(
    "see-declaration-of",
    -1,
    "see declaration of '~decl:Decl'",
    span { loc = "decl:Decl" }
)

standalone_note(
    "see-declaration-of-interface-requirement",
    -1,
    "see interface requirement declaration of '~decl:Decl'",
    span { loc = "decl:Decl" }
)

standalone_note(
    "see-overload-considered",
    -1,
    "see overloads considered: '~decl:Decl'.",
    span { loc = "decl:Decl" }
)

standalone_note(
    "see-previous-declaration-of",
    -1,
    "see previous declaration of '~decl:Decl'",
    span { loc = "decl:Decl" }
)

standalone_note(
    "note-explicit-conversion-possible",
    -1,
    "explicit conversion from '~fromType:Type' to '~toType:Type' is possible",
    span { loc = "location" }
)

-- IR-specific variants for use in IR linking and lowering passes
-- These take IRInst* instead of Decl*

standalone_note(
    "see-declaration-of-ir",
    -1,
    "see declaration of '~inst:IRInst'",
    span { loc = "inst:IRInst" }
)

standalone_note(
    "see-call-of-func-ir",
    -1,
    "see call to '~inst:IRInst'",
    span { loc = "location" }
)

-- Modifier variant for notes
standalone_note(
    "see-declaration-of-modifier",
    -1,
    "see declaration of '~modifier:Modifier'",
    span { loc = "modifier:Modifier" }
)

-- ASTNodeType variant for generic references
standalone_note(
    "see-using-of-node-type",
    -1,
    "see using of '~nodeType'",
    span { loc = "location" }
)


-- Process and validate all diagnostics
processed_diagnostics, validation_errors = helpers.process_diagnostics(helpers.diagnostics)

if #validation_errors > 0 then
    error("Diagnostic validation failed:\n" .. table.concat(validation_errors, "\n"))
end

return processed_diagnostics

thought.text: I’ll mark implementation and verification progress in the task plan.

action.command: plan

task_list.title: Phase 1: Reword and understand issue
task_list.notes: Issue: generic RWByteAddressBuffer.Load<T>/Store<T> currently accepts interface/existential T such as IFoo, then later emits/crashes on illegal IR (lookupWitness) or silently fails on CPU. Repro uses [anyValueSize(8)] interface IFoo, ImplA:IFoo, byteBuffer.Store<IFoo>, byteBuffer.Load<IFoo>, and loaded.getValue(). Expected is correct serialization or clear diagnostic.

task_list.title: Phase 2: Discover and run relevant tests
task_list.notes: Found README/CONTRIBUTING guidance: build exists under build/Release/bin; slang-test runs from repo root. Existing regression file is disabled, so targeted slang-test reports no tests run. Environment otherwise has slangc/slang-test ready.

task_list.title: Phase 3: Explore related compiler code
task_list.notes: Relevant files: hlsl.meta.slang declares generic ByteAddressBuffer intrinsics; slang-ir-byte-address-legalize.cpp lowers byte-address ops but runs after specialization; slang-emit.cpp schedules specializeModule before byte-address legalization; slang-ir-typeflow-specialize.cpp crashes while resolving types for the interface-specialized Load generic. Existing StructuredBuffer support works through dynamic-dispatch/existential lowering.

task_list.title: Phase 4: Create temporary reproduction script
task_list.notes: Created /tmp/byteaddress-interface-repro.slang. Current build segfaults for HLSL/SPIR-V at specializeModule before byte-address legalization; -no-codegen succeeds, confirming front-end accepts it.

task_list.title: Phase 5: Analyze fix strategy
task_list.notes: Minimal compliant fix: emit a clear diagnostic before specialization when ByteAddressBuffer Load/Store is used with an interface type, preventing crash/ICE and CPU silent failure. Implemented a precise pre-specialization scanner for direct byteAddressBufferLoad/store and Load/Store generics where the byte-address operation's element type maps to IRInterfaceType.

task_list.title: Phase 6: Implement focused fix
task_list.notes: Added E41301 diagnostic, diagnoseByteAddressBufferInterfaceTypes pass, and scheduled it before specializeModule.

task_list.title: Phase 7: Verify thoroughly
task_list.notes: Built local slangc; verified HLSL/SPIR-V/host-cpp repro now emits E41301, load-only and store-only edge cases diagnose, non-interface ByteAddressBuffer struct compiles, StructuredBuffer<IFoo> compiles, unrelated generic IFoo with Load<uint> is not rejected, and existing byte-address struct test compiles.

task_list.title: Phase 8: Final review
task_list.notes: Review diff against base and ensure no test modifications.

thought.text: I’ll provide a concise summary of changes and verification.

action.message:
Implemented the minimal non-test fix for the issue.

Summary:
- Added a new clear diagnostic `E41301` for interface-typed `ByteAddressBuffer`/`RWByteAddressBuffer` `Load<T>` and `Store<T>` usage.
- Added `diagnoseByteAddressBufferInterfaceTypes()` in `slang-ir-byte-address-legalize.cpp/.h`.
- Scheduled that diagnostic pass before `specializeModule` in `slang-emit.cpp`, so the compiler reports a user-facing error before the previous specialization/codegen crash path.
- Did not modify any tests.

Changed files:
- `source/slang/slang-diagnostics.lua`
- `source/slang/slang-emit.cpp`
- `source/slang/slang-ir-byte-address-legalize.cpp`
- `source/slang/slang-ir-byte-address-legalize.h`

Behavior now:
- The reproducer no longer ICEs/segfaults for HLSL, SPIR-V, or host C++ targets.
- It emits clear diagnostics such as:
  `interface type 'IFoo' is not supported for ByteAddressBuffer Load/Store. Use StructuredBuffer/RWStructuredBuffer for interface-typed values, or serialize a concrete representation explicitly.`

Verification performed with a local rebuilt `slangc` (`build-local/Release/bin/slangc`):
- Reproducer: HLSL, SPIR-V, and host-cpp all emit `E41301` instead of crashing.
- Load-only and store-only interface edge cases both emit `E41301`.
- Non-interface `RWByteAddressBuffer.Store<Data>` / `Load<Data>` still compiles.
- Existing `StructuredBuffer<IFoo>` / `RWStructuredBuffer<IFoo>` compile test still compiles.
- A generic `helper<IFoo>()` that performs unrelated `Load<uint>` is not falsely rejected.
- Existing non-interface ByteAddressBuffer struct test compiles.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Compared against base `ef3d763e9a2d3fa87e4c002906a952895f363fe6`; only the four non-test source files above are changed.

content.text:
Implemented the minimal non-test fix for the issue.

Summary:
- Added a new clear diagnostic `E41301` for interface-typed `ByteAddressBuffer`/`RWByteAddressBuffer` `Load<T>` and `Store<T>` usage.
- Added `diagnoseByteAddressBufferInterfaceTypes()` in `slang-ir-byte-address-legalize.cpp/.h`.
- Scheduled that diagnostic pass before `specializeModule` in `slang-emit.cpp`, so the compiler reports a user-facing error before the previous specialization/codegen crash path.
- Did not modify any tests.

Changed files:
- `source/slang/slang-diagnostics.lua`
- `source/slang/slang-emit.cpp`
- `source/slang/slang-ir-byte-address-legalize.cpp`
- `source/slang/slang-ir-byte-address-legalize.h`

Behavior now:
- The reproducer no longer ICEs/segfaults for HLSL, SPIR-V, or host C++ targets.
- It emits clear diagnostics such as:
  `interface type 'IFoo' is not supported for ByteAddressBuffer Load/Store. Use StructuredBuffer/RWStructuredBuffer for interface-typed values, or serialize a concrete representation explicitly.`

Verification performed with a local rebuilt `slangc` (`build-local/Release/bin/slangc`):
- Reproducer: HLSL, SPIR-V, and host-cpp all emit `E41301` instead of crashing.
- Load-only and store-only interface edge cases both emit `E41301`.
- Non-interface `RWByteAddressBuffer.Store<Data>` / `Load<Data>` still compiles.
- Existing `StructuredBuffer<IFoo>` / `RWStructuredBuffer<IFoo>` compile test still compiles.
- A generic `helper<IFoo>()` that performs unrelated `Load<uint>` is not falsely rejected.
- Existing non-interface ByteAddressBuffer struct test compiles.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- Compared against base `ef3d763e9a2d3fa87e4c002906a952895f363fe6`; only the four non-test source files above are changed.
